title: `docker --host unix:///podman.sock build` using local image as base reports `insufficient_scope: authorization failed`
body:
### Issue Description

Using the docker CLI to connect to the podman socket to build an image that uses a local image as a base image, one encounters an authorisation error.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```
# 1. Fine: build docker image from docker CLI with registry image as base
sudo docker build -f - -t a --load . <<EOF
FROM fedora
EOF

# 2. Fine: build docker image from docker CLI with local image as base
sudo docker build -f - -t b --load . <<EOF
FROM a
EOF

# 3. Fine: build podman image from docker CLI with registry image as base
docker --host unix:///run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock build -f - -t a --load . <<EOF
FROM fedora
EOF

# 4. FAILS: build podman image from docker CLI with local image as base
docker --host unix:///run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock build -f - -t b --load . <<EOF
FROM a
EOF

# 5. Fine: build podman image from podman CLI with local image as base
podman build  -f - -t b --load . <<EOF
FROM a
EOF

# 6. Fine: run local podman image from docker CLI
docker --host unix:///run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock run -it a bash
```

### Describe the results you received

Output of step 4:

```
[+] Building 3.0s (3/3) FINISHED
 => [internal] load .dockerignore                                                                      0.1s 
 => => transferring context: 2B                                                                        0.0s 
 => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile                                                   0.0s 
 => => transferring dockerfile: 111B                                                                   0.0s 
 => ERROR [internal] load metadata for docker.io/library/a:latest                                      2.8s 
------
 > [internal] load metadata for docker.io/library/a:latest:
------
Dockerfile:1
--------------------
   1 | >>> FROM a
   2 |
--------------------
ERROR: failed to solve: a: pull access denied, repository does not exist or may require authorization: server message: insufficient_scope: authorization failed
```

### Describe the results you expected

Building a podman image from the docker CLI using a local image as base should succeed, in the same way building a podman image from the podman CLI succeeds, and building a docker image from the docker CLI succeeds.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out and for providing the producer, @scottcwang.

@flouthoc, do you have time to look into it?  I think it has something to do with how the tag is being interpreted on the remote end.  By default, the tag should default to `docker.io` and not localhost.

@scottcwang Which `docker` client are you using this is working fine for me, also what is `--load` in your commands its not a valid flag for `build` command including both `docker` and `podman`.

```console
[fl@fedora bin]$ sudo docker --host unix:///run/podman/podman.sock build -f - -t a . <<EOF
FROM fedora
EOF
Sending build context to Docker daemon  105.3MB
STEP 1/1: FROM fedora
Resolving %!q(<nil>) to docker.io (enforced by caller)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/fedora:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:f71a5d924485b230392e05b88ef566c057cb4e7daf651408032e50bc735f54d7
Copying config sha256:ad2032316c2664fe02873afaf98e6ab5323d1980d4b99d8de55848cd6ffae1f8
Writing manifest to image destination
COMMIT docker.io/library/a
--> ad2032316c26
Successfully tagged docker.io/library/a:latest
Successfully tagged docker.io/library/fedora:latest
ad2032316c2664fe02873afaf98e6ab5323d1980d4b99d8de55848cd6ffae1f8
Successfully built ad2032316c26
Successfully tagged a
```

Then using `a` as base image

```console
[fl@fedora bin]$ sudo docker --host unix:///run/podman/podman.sock build -f - -t b . <<EOF
FROM a     
EOF
Sending build context to Docker daemon  105.3MB
STEP 1/1: FROM a
COMMIT docker.io/library/b
--> ad2032316c26
Successfully tagged docker.io/library/b:latest
Successfully tagged docker.io/library/a:latest
Successfully tagged docker.io/library/fedora:latest
ad2032316c2664fe02873afaf98e6ab5323d1980d4b99d8de55848cd6ffae1f8
Successfully built ad2032316c26
Successfully tagged b
```

Ah I see, the issue you are hitting, it happens when we are using buildkit, unfortunately afaics buildkit is using different API from the public API exposed by docker here: https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.43/#tag/Image/operation/ImageBuild

Issue is happening when buildkit client is trying to load image metadata from `podman-remote`' API. This is not yet implemented on `podman` end and in-order to have this on podman we will need public API comapat to work with buildkit. 

Podman supports compat only on the public-ally exposed API ( https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.43/#tag/Image/operation/ImageBuild ). I think switching to legacy docker build will work just fine for you.

Please feel free to comment or re-open the issue.

I hope above workaround works for you and buildkit compat API is a bigger RFE that needs to be tracked separately than this issue.
You can track buildkit RFE here: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17836
